content: read reviews/781.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/781.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/781.md.

    45→
    46→The original conjecture was "f(k) = k² − k + 1 for all k", which was **disproved** by Alon–Spencer. The formalization captures this as a **direct assertion** (Case 3): the Alon–Spencer lower bound f(k) ≥ C·k³, which is a stronger positive result implying the negation of the original conjecture.

file.content:
# Review: deepmind/781.lean — Erdős Problem 781

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports` at line 17.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line (line 19).
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 781`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/781`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (descending waves, 2-colourings, and the function f(k)).
- [x] Bibliography entries present: `[BEF90]` (Brown–Erdős–Freedman) and `[AlSp89]` (Alon–Spencer).

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos781 ... end Erdos781`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos781`).
- [x] No `open`/`open scoped` statements needed (no Mathlib namespaces opened).

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `IsDescendingWave` (line 47): has a LaTeX docstring (lines 42–46). Prop type, `UpperCamelCase`. No `sorry`.
- [x] `HasMonochromaticDescendingWave` (line 54): has a LaTeX docstring (lines 52–53). Prop type, `UpperCamelCase`. No `sorry`.
- [x] `descendingWaveNumber` (line 60): has a LaTeX docstring (lines 58–59). Returns `ℕ`, `lowerCamelCase`. Marked `noncomputable` (uses `sInf`). No `sorry`.
- [x] No problem-specific notation needed; none used.
- [ ] No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks for definitions. *(Optional — not required.)*

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] All benchmark problems use the `theorem` keyword (`erdos_781`, two variants).
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_781` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] All names follow `snake_case` convention.
- [x] Each theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**.
- [x] Main theorem and both variants have docstrings.
- [x] Docstrings reference source papers: main theorem references `[AlSp89]`, variants reference `[BEF90]`.

### `answer()` usage

The original conjecture was "f(k) = k² − k + 1 for all k", which was **disproved** by Alon–Spencer. The formalization captures this as a **direct assertion** (Case 3): the Alon–Spencer lower bound f(k) ≥ C·k³, which is a stronger positive result implying the negation of the original conjecture.

- [x] Case 3 applies — the theorem states a direct assertion (`∃ C > 0, ...`), no `answer()` wrapper needed.
- [x] No quantifiers improperly placed as theorem parameters on the main theorem (quantifiers are inside the statement).

**Note:** The variants `bef_lower_bound` and `bef_upper_bound` take `(k : ℕ)` as a theorem parameter. This is acceptable since they do not use `answer()` — the parameter restriction applies only to `answer()`-wrapped theorems.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Every theorem has exactly one `@[category ...]`.
- [x] All three use `category research solved` — appropriate since the Alon–Spencer and Brown–Erdős–Freedman bounds are proven results (not yet formally verified in Lean).
- [x] `research solved` theorems may contain `sorry` (the restriction on `sorry`-free proofs applies only to `research open`).

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Every theorem has exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `5` (combinatorics).
- [x] Numbers in ascending order (trivially, only one number).
- [x] No duplicates.
- [x] `5` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] `erdos_781`: uses `by\n  sorry` (multi-line form, acceptable).
- [x] `erdos_781.variants.bef_lower_bound`: uses `by\n  sorry`.
- [x] `erdos_781.variants.bef_upper_bound`: uses `by\n  sorry`.
- [x] No `sorry` in any `def`/`abbrev`/`structure` declaration.
- [x] No informal proof sketches (problems are `research solved`, not `research open`).
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- [x] Variants named `erdos_781.variants.bef_lower_bound` and `erdos_781.variants.bef_upper_bound`.
- [x] All variants live in the same file as the main theorem.
- [x] Each variant has its own `@[category research solved, AMS 5]` attributes.
- [x] Both variants have brief docstrings explaining their mathematical content.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `∀`, `∃`, `∈` (implicit), `≥`, `→`, `ℕ`, `ℝ`, `Fin`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (longest line ~80 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` — not verified in this review.
- [ ] Custom linters — not verified in this review.

---

## Summary

**Status: PASS**

The file is clean and well-structured. All required checklist items pass. Three custom definitions (`IsDescendingWave`, `HasMonochromaticDescendingWave`, `descendingWaveNumber`) are properly documented, correctly cased, and `noncomputable` where needed. The main theorem captures the Alon–Spencer disproof as a direct assertion (Case 3), which is the appropriate formalization since the result is a positive bound rather than a yes/no answer. Variants for the original Brown–Erdős–Freedman bounds are correctly namespaced and attributed. Only build verification remains unconfirmed.

content.text: The review passed (**Status: PASS**). No changes needed.
